english: mdzá-ba-rnams (mdza' ba rnams) a loving married couple Dzl.; mdza-żiṅ sdúg-par gyúr-ba (mdza' zhing sdug par gyur ba) loving each other, e.g. like brothers or sisters, Dzl.; mi-mdzá-ba tams-cád (mi mdza' ba thams cad) any hostile, malignant (creatures or powers) Dom.; mi-mdzá-ba-rnams sdúm-pa (mi mdza' ba rnams sdum pa) to reconcile those that are at variance Thgy.; brám-ze mdzá-żiṅ śés-pa żig yód-de (bram ze mdza' zhing shes pa zhig yod de) he had a Brahmin for his intimate friend Dzl.; mdza-bśés (mdza' bshes) friend, frq. in conjunction with nyé-du (nye du) or kyim-mtses (khyim mtshes) Glr.; mdzá-bo (mdza' bo) id. Dzl. etc. and vulgo, rarely mdzao (mdza'o) Thgy.; still more vulg. Ts.: *dzán-te* (*dzan te*), *dzá-mo* (*dza' mo*) fem.; *dzá-wo jhe-pa* (*dza' wo jhe pa*), C., = mdzá-ba (mdza' ba); mdza-grógs (mdza' grogs) intimate friend Sch.; C.: husband, wife.

1. wise, learned, frq.; mkás-śiṅ mdzáṅs-pa (mkhas shing mdzangs pa), ytsug-lag-će-żiṅ mdzáṅs-pa (gtsug lag che zhing mdzangs pa); mdzáṅs-blun (mdzangs blun) the wise man and the fool, a relig. composition, publ. by Schmidt, together with a German translation, containing an endless variety of examples relative to the Buddhist doctrine of future rewards and punishments; mdzáṅs-ma (mdzangs ma) a wise woman Glr. - 2. gentle, noble, distinguished as to rank, ya-rábs mdzáṅs-kyi bu (ya rabs mdzangs kyi bu) Glr. po. - (The spelling dzáṅs-pa (dzangs pa) is not of unfrequent occurrence, but seems to be objectionable.)

imp. mdzód (mdzod) (W. also *dzad* (*dzad*)), to do, to act, resp. for byéd-pa (byed pa) in all its significations, whenever the person acting is the object of respect, hence almost without exception with regard to Buddha; but also in common life: *ći dzad dug* (*ci dzad dug*) W. what is your honour doing? also together with byed-pa (byed pa), grógs byéd-par mdzód ċig (grogs byed par mdzod cig) pray, help me! further as a sbst.: the act of doing, the thing done, the deed, mdzád-pa bċu-gnyís (mdzad pa bcu gnyis) the twelve deeds (or prop. incidents) of an incarnated Buddha, viz. the descending from the gods, conception, birth, exhibition of skill (i.e. going through certain chivalrous exercises), conjugal diversion, relinquishing family-ties, engaging in penitential exercises, conquering the devil, becoming Buddha, preaching, dying, being deposited in the shape of relics; sometimes even hundred (or rather 125) such deeds are enumerated Cs. - Comp. and deriv. mdzád(-pa)-po (mdzad pa po) a maker, composer etc.; also to be used for creator. - mdzad-spyód (mdzad spyod) resp. deed, action Mil.; deportment, conduct, like spyód-lam (spyod lam) Mil.; course of life, way of acting, e.g. of a heretical king Pth.
Ld. pitted with the small-pox, pock-marked; warty, blotchy, v. mdzér-pa (mdzer pa).

, vulgo མཛུག་གུ་ mdzúg-gu (mdzug gu), 1. finger, esp. fore-finger; tams-ćád kár mdzúb-mo ćúg-la sdod (thams cad khar mdzub mo chug la sdod) Glr. now sit down and put your finger into your mouth (for our: put your finger upon your mouth), i.e. be silent, as becomes the vanquished; *dzúg-gu tị'-pa* (*dzug gu ti' pa*) C. a kind of covenanting, the two parties wetting their fingers with saliva and then striking them against one another, which ceremony is considered more stringent than that of *rdo ćóg-pa* (*rdo cog pa*), v. rdo (rdo). The different fingers are: (m)té-bo ((m)the bo), (m)téb-mo ((m)theb mo) thumb; mdzúb-mo (mdzub mo) B., *dzúg-gu* (*dzug gu*) vulgo, ston-byéd (ston byed) Cs., mtsod (mtsod) Med. fore-finger; srin-lád (srin lad), bar-mdzúb (bar mdzub) Cs., *gúṅ-dzug* (*gung dzug*) C., kán-ma (kan ma) Med. middle-finger; srin-mdzub (srin mdzub) Cs., *srin-dzug* (*srin dzug*) vulgo, miṅ-méd (ming med) (Cs., acc. to Ssk.) ćad (chad) Med. the fourth finger; (m)te(-ba) ((m)the (-ba)) or teu-ćúṅ (the'u chung), *dzug-ćúṅ* (*dzug chung*) C. the little finger. - 2. toe. - 3. claw. Comp. mdzub-kér, -kyér or -kyáṅ (mdzub ker, -kyer or -kyang) Cs. a stiff finger. - mdzub-brkyáṅs (mdzub brkyangs) Cs. an extended finger. - mdzub-skyis (mdzub skyis) finger-ring (= ser-gdub (ser gdub)) Lew. - mdzub-krid (mdzub khrid) a pointing with the finger, hint, intimation, direction, blo-té-tsom sél-bai mdzub-krid byas (blo the tshom sel ba'i mdzub khrid byas) he made an intimation that removed every scruple of the mind Glr. - *dzug-gáṅ* (*dzug gang*) W. a span, measured with thumb and fore-finger. - mdzub-gúg (mdzub gug) a crooked finger Cs. - *mdzub-rtín* (*mdzub rting*) vulgo, thimble - mdzub-mtó (mdzub mtho) 'a span measured with the thumb and middle-finger' Sch. prob. = mdzug-gaṅ (mdzug gang). - mdzub-rdúb (mdzub rdub) a mutilated finger Cs. - mdzub-brdá (mdzub brda) a hint or sign given with a finger Cs. - mdzub-rtsé (mdzub rtse) tip of a finger Cs. - mdzub-tsigs (mdzub tshigs) joint of
